item.description:
## CueNow 1.0.6
### Fixes - **Note History:** The archived notes grid now fills the available space and scrolls on its own, with the search bar and action buttons pinned in place. Previously the list was clipped to a single row. - **Sticky note text rendering:** Fixed an issue where typed characters could be invisible in notes on macOS 13–15 (the text was present but not drawn). Notes now render reliably across macOS versions.
### New - **Hide dock icon:** Added a "Hide dock icon" toggle under Configuration → General. Turn it on to run CueNow purely from the menu bar. The setting applies immediately and persists across launches.

- Saves the text you're actively typing in a note before quitting, archiving, deleting, or running an in-app update — so your latest words are never left behind. - The Support item in the menubar dropdown now opens Settings directly on the Support tab. - Improved note storage path handling for broader macOS version compatibility, and made pre-update backup failures non-blocking so a backup hiccup can't stop your notes from being saved.

- Fixed note persistence when reopening CueNow after quitting the app. - Fixed storage path handling so notes in `Application Support/CueNow` are loaded correctly instead of being replaced by a blank note. - Added regression coverage for note storage paths containing spaces.
